summary: Strong momentum day. IM caught the NY session breakout perfectly. Entry at 0.55×ATR threshold triggered clean, rode trend for +2.67R. Memory recall correctly identified similar setups from Feb 24.

VB London breakout textbook execution. Range compression during Asia (ATR < 0.35×D1) set up high-probability breakout. +1.90R with clean SL placement below range. Two strategy coordination worked well.
IM NY entry was good (+2.67R) but early partial exit left money on table. Momentum continued another 45 pips after exit. Need to review trailing stop logic for strong trend days.
Mixed day across strategies. VB Asia entry was premature (-0.92R), but IM recovery trade was solid. Net positive but could improve entry timing during low-volatility sessions.
PB London entry was technically correct but market reversed immediately after entry. Stopped out at -0.47R. Regime was transitioning from trending to ranging — memory should have flagged this.
Best day of the week. IM NY session caught strong momentum move (+2.47R). OWM recall confidence was 0.78 — correctly identified this as a high-probability pattern from similar Feb setups.
VB London was clean (+1.15R). PB Asia entry questionable — spread gate almost blocked it. Consider tightening Asia session criteria. Overall positive day with good risk management.

Worst day this month. IM London entry against trend (-0.85R), followed by PB NY loss (-1.04R from previous day's setup). Two consecutive losses exposed correlation risk between strategies in ranging market.
